issuer.display_name: AgentMarshal seed contracts (v0.2 Bubble 17 — BD MCP proxy)

declared_scope.description: Trading-desk contract: gates the trade lifecycle (propose_trade, execute_trade, run_risk_check, fetch_research) for the TradingAgent / ResearchAgent / RiskAgent / ExecutionAgent fleet. The entity_not_sanctioned composite screens each action's counterparty/target entity against the OFAC SDN list supplied at runtime via action_properties.regulatory_state.ofac_sdn_list. PRE-scaffold: the composite returns an unresolved stub when the SDN list is not injected; the live OFAC SDN feed via Bright Data replaces the fixture-injected stub in a later workstream. The entity_adverse_media_check composite (Bubble 19) screens the same counterparty for adverse media by chaining a governed SERP search and Crawl API extraction through the MCP proxy and scoring the extracted article content against a financial-crime keyword list, returning pass, review, or fail; it is best-effort (non-blocking) when the screening provider is unavailable. Beyond declared_scope, this contract's bd_permissions govern the Bright Data screening pipeline the fleet drives through the MCP proxy: counterparty adverse media via SERP, Web Unlocker (paywalled financial news), and the Crawl API (article extraction). Beneficial-owner registry lookups via the Scraping Browser are planned for a later bubble.

decision.reason: Trading desk v1 composite checks passed; entity not on the injected OFAC SDN list.

bd_permissions.description: SERP search for counterparty adverse news on financial-crime keywords
Web Unlocker bypass for paywalled financial news during adverse media review
Crawl API extraction of article content from SERP-discovered URLs for adverse media analysis
Governance-wrapped passthrough to Bright Data's hosted MCP server; forwards tools/call only for the curated web-access tools, so any future BD MCP tool is auto-governed without code changes
